cinematic_pitch: Conquer the Dolomites' towering peaks and razor-sharp alpine passes where every curve commands respect and every summit offers a breathtaking canvas of rugged grandeur. This is a realm where precision riding meets awe-inspiring vistas, inviting the elite rider to master roads carved through the highest mountains of Austria's Glockner and Venediger Groups.
ride_character: An advanced mountain asphalt ascent, the Dolomites challenge riders with steep gradients, technical switchbacks, and a relentless elevation that demands focus and rewards with unparalleled panoramic views.
best_season: June through September — the passes shed their snow and the alpine environment bursts into vivid clarity, offering optimal conditions for both riding and sightseeing.

premium_pois.name: Grossglockner
premium_pois.cinematic_description: Rising to 3798 meters, Grossglockner dominates the horizon as Austria's highest peak, a monumental presence that defines the ride and beckons riders to witness its rugged majesty from every vantage point.
premium_pois.rider_tip: Prepare for rapidly changing weather conditions at altitude and ensure your bike's brakes are in peak condition for steep descents.

premium_pois.name: Obere Glocknerscharte
premium_pois.cinematic_description: This alpine pass at 3756 meters slices through the Glockner Group, delivering a high-altitude gateway framed by jagged ridges and ice-sculpted rock faces, a true test of skill and nerve.
premium_pois.rider_tip: Approach with caution — the pass demands precise throttle control and attention to road surface changes on the narrow asphalt segments.

premium_pois.name: Großvenediger
premium_pois.cinematic_description: At 3657 meters, Großvenediger is a towering sentinel in the Venediger Group, its austere glaciers and sheer faces offering an imposing backdrop as you navigate the challenging mountain asphalt roads.
premium_pois.rider_tip: Plan fuel stops carefully and be mindful of thinner air affecting engine performance at this elevation.
